Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 102. Towing Logs (ABL 0398)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na] LUGAL EN-⸢ia⸣

(1) [To] the king, m[y] lord: your [servant] Ṭab-ṣill-E[šarra]. Good health to the king, [my] lord! May Aššur and Mullissu bless the king, [my lord]!

o 22

[ARAD]-ka mDÙG.GA—ṣil—⸢É⸣.[ŠÁR.RA]

o 33

⸢lu DI⸣-mu a-na LUGAL EN-[ia]

o 44

aš-šur dNIN.LÍL a-na LUGAL [EN-ia]

o 55

lik-ru-bu ina UGU GIŠ.⸢ÙR⸣-[MEŠ]

(5) As to the logs which the king, my lord, wrote me about: "Send (word) that they be towed here,"

o 66

ša LUGAL be-lí iš-pur-an-[ni]

o 77

ma-a šu-pur liš-da-du-[u-ni]

o 88

a-du la-a LUGAL be-lí iš-[pur-an-ni]

(8) even before the king, my lord, wr[ote to me], I assigned [......

o 99

[x x]+⸢x⸣ ú-ta-si-[ik]

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334273/.
